specification.topic-statement: The Anthropic response builder assembles the final in-memory response structure from streaming state accumulated during a completed Anthropic streaming response, applying strict content block ordering and defaulting rules.

scope.in-scope: Content block ordering rules, tool call creation requirements, empty argument defaulting, optional thinking signature handling, text block exclusion from final response, and streaming accumulation patterns for thinking text, thinking signature, and tool arguments.
scope.boundary: How streaming events are parsed from the Anthropic SSE stream and dispatched as normalized internal events is covered by the provider serialization specification.
Translation of Anthropic stop_reason strings to the internal finish reason enumeration is covered by the finish reason mapping specification.
Budget and mode selection for extended thinking requests is covered by the Anthropic thinking specification.

behavior.streaming-accumulation:
During streaming, three pieces of state are accumulated for later assembly into the final response:
Thinking text is accumulated by appending each thinking delta to the current thinking text buffer. Multiple deltas across the stream are concatenated in order.
When a thinking signature delta arrives, it replaces the current signature value rather than appending to it. Only the most recent signature delta is retained. If no signature delta is ever received, the signature field remains null.
Tool argument JSON fragments are accumulated by appending each input_json_delta to the current tool arguments buffer. Multiple deltas across the stream are concatenated in order.
behavior.tool-state-preservation: When a content_block_stop event is received during streaming, the accumulated tool state (tool ID, tool name, tool arguments) is preserved for use by the response builder. This state remains accessible after the stop event and is consumed when the final response is assembled.
behavior.content-block-ordering:
The final response content block array is constructed in strict fixed order. Blocks are appended to the array in the following sequence, skipping any block type that has no accumulated state:
1. Thinking block (if thinking text was accumulated) 2. Redacted thinking block (if redacted thinking data was accumulated) 3. Tool call block (if both a tool ID and tool name are present)
Text content blocks are not materialized in the final response structure. Text deltas are emitted as streaming events for display purposes but are not collected into the response content block array. The final response contains only thinking, redacted thinking, and tool call blocks.
behavior.thinking-block-construction: A thinking block is added to the response when thinking text was accumulated during streaming. The block contains the accumulated thinking text and the signature value. If no signature delta was received during the stream, the signature field is null.
behavior.tool-call-block-construction:
A tool call block is added to the response only when both a tool ID and a tool name are present in the accumulated state. If either the tool ID or the tool name is absent, no tool call block is created and the accumulated tool state is silently discarded.
When a tool call block is created and the accumulated tool arguments buffer is null or empty, the arguments field is set to the string "{}" rather than null or an empty string. This ensures a valid JSON object is always present in the tool call arguments field.
